[JS Weird World] No.52 A Dangerous Little Reminder: `new` and Functions
JsWeirdWorld
JavaScript 全攻略:克服JS奇怪的部分

What happens if you forget the `new` in front of a function constructor? `this` ends up pointing at `window`, the function returns `undefined`, and every prototype method becomes unreachable. This post explains why the industry convention is to capitalize the first letter of a constructor.
/en/jsweirdworld/2019/05/22/js-weird-world-51-function-constructors-prototype/
[JS Weird World] No.51 Function Constructors and `.prototype`

How does a function constructor wire up an object's prototype? This post starts from `john.__proto__` and walks through writing something like `Person.prototype.getFullName` — how that hangs a method off the shared prototype object that every instance gets to use. We'll also see why putting shared methods on the prototype beats stuffing them into the constructor.
/en/jsweirdworld/2019/05/22/jsweirdworld-50-function-constructors-new-history/
[JS Weird World] No.50 Function Constructors, `new`, and a Bit of JavaScript History

What does the `new` keyword in JavaScript actually do? This post starts with the history — `new` was added to make Java developers feel at home — and then digs into operator precedence to show how `new` changes what `this` points to, and how function constructors build objects.
/en/jsweirdworld/2019/05/22/js-weird-world-49-reflection-and-extend/
[JS Weird World] No.49 Reflection and Extend

What is Reflection? And why do so many libraries lean on extend? This post starts from for...in walking the prototype chain, then shows how a JavaScript object can observe and modify itself through Reflection — and how underscore's extend pulls multiple objects together.
/en/jsweirdworld/2019/05/21/js-weird-world-48-everything-is-an-object/
[JS Weird World] No.48 Everything Is an Object (or a Primitive)

Why do people say everything in JavaScript is an object? Arrays have a prototype, functions have a prototype, and even numbers and strings do too. In this post we follow __proto__ all the way down the prototype chain to the base object sitting at the very bottom.

[JS Weird World] No.47 Understanding Prototypes
JavaScript Full Guide: Conquering the Weird Parts of JS

What exactly is a prototype in JavaScript? How do __proto__ and the prototype chain actually work? This post starts from how the dot operator looks things up, and walks you through how the prototype chain links objects together one by one.
/en/jsweirdworld/2019/05/21/jsweirdworld-46-classical-vs-prototypal-inheritance/
[JS Weird World] No.46 Quick Concept Check: Classical vs Prototypal Inheritance

How is JavaScript's prototypal inheritance different from the classical inheritance you see in Java or C#? No car-and-train analogies here. This post gets straight to what inheritance actually is: one object using another object's properties and methods.
/en/jsweirdworld/2019/05/21/jsweirdworld-45-objects-functions-chapter-wrapup/
[JS Weird World] No.45 Wrapping Up the Objects & Functions Chapter

A quick recap note after finishing the whole Objects & Functions chapter in JavaScript. IIFEs, closures, functions-are-objects, and automatic semicolon insertion — once these clicked, it's time to head into the world of prototype inheritance.

[JS Weird World] No.44 Functional Programming (Part 2)

Once you've got the functional programming concepts down, where do you go next? This post points you at two classic JavaScript utility libraries — underscore.js and Lodash. The idea is to read the open-source code and work backwards from it, until the concepts turn into muscle memory.
/en/jsweirdworld/2019/05/21/jsweirdworld-43-functional-programming-01/
[JS Weird World] No.43 Functional Programming (Part 1)

Why is JavaScript closer to Lisp and Scheme than to Java? In this post we use first-class functions to build a little mapForEach, and walk through how functional programming helps you wrap up repetitive work into something clean and reusable so you write less and do more.
